llm.summary: The RCA task satisfies its blocker-specific exit criteria. The original repeated failure is documented as an environment-specific Inspector CLI path issue, first from npm registry lookup failure and then loopback EPERM behavior. The exact required Inspector command now passes in the current workspace and returns the seeded server_status resource. The RCA plan has next_task_on_success set to mcp-resources-runtime, the parent task remains present and blocked with this RCA as its blocker, and the parent progress log records the resolution before return.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Repeated blocker was reproduced and explained with concrete evidence: ENOTFOUND registry.npmjs.org before any MCP request, followed by loopback/listen/connect EPERM after cached Inspector resolution.

Required command passed during evaluation: npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/inspector@0.21.2 --cli http://127.0.0.1:3100/mcp/none --transport http --method resources/list returned the seeded server_status resource with exit code 0.
RCA task metadata supports normal promotion return via next_task_on_success: mcp-resources-runtime and rca_for_task_id: mcp-resources-runtime.
Parent task log records the blocker resolution and the exact restored command path.
Implementation is scoped to the Inspector blocker path: local Inspector shim plus narrow loopback POST handling for /mcp/none.
